Path LMS Reporting API

Welcome to the Blue Sky eLearn Path LMS Reporting API documentation page. We're excited to announce our latest project that expands on the existing Path LMS reporting capabilities.

Overview

The Path LMS Reporting API is a GraphQL-based API designed to provide efficient data processing and flexible querying capabilities. Our ultimate goal with this is initiative is to provide our clients a more efficient way to retrieve data from Path LMS. With the Path LMS Reporting API you will be able to create queries to fetch the data that you are interested in without receiving data fields that you may not need. In addition to specifying the data fields that you need, you are also able to explore relationships between different data points within the system and fetch data points not immediately available through the Path LMS Reports user interface.

Authentication

Base URL

Before we discuss authentication we should discuss a key difference between RESTful APIs and a GraphQL API. While RESTful APIs have different endpoints that correspond to different objects, a GraphQL API has one endpoint that can consume many different queries. With that said, we have added three different endpoints to expand the Reporting API capabilities.

When making requests to the Reporting API, you will need to use the base URL to make your queries.

Base URL

https://data-api.pathlms.com

Generate Access Token Endpoint:

POST {{baseURL}}/api/v1/getToken

GraphQL Query Endpoint:

POST {{baseURL}}/graphql

Generate CSV File Endpoint:

POST {{baseURL}}/api/v1/csv

These are the main routes are the ones that you will be using to interact with the Reporting API.

Generating an Authentication Token

To leverage the Path LMS Reporting API you will need an authentication token that will allow you to make requests to the reporting API. To generate the authentication token you will need to request a Client ID and a Client Secret from the Blue Sky eLearn team. To request this token, please reach out to your CSM.

For additional information on how to access receive access to the Path LMS Reporting API, please click here.

Once you have received a Client ID and a Client Secret you can make a request to our authentication endpoint to receive a bearer token that can be used for authentication.

Endpoint:

  POST {{baseURL}}/api/v1/getToken

Sample Request:

  curl --location '{{baseURL}}/api/v1/getToken' \
  --header 'Authorization;' \
  --header 'Content-Type: application/x-www-form-urlencoded' \
  --data-urlencode 'applicationId={{ClientID}}' \
  --data-urlencode 'applicationSecret={{ClientSecret}}'

Sample Response:

{
  "token": "Bearer yourBearerToken"
}

Note: The bearer token that is returned is valid for 12 hours from the time you generate the token. Once the token has expired, you will need to generate a new token.

Sample Request Using Postman

Note: Your request should contain a x-www-form-urlencoded payload with an applicationId and an applicationSecret key.

Using your Bearer Token

To successfully make requests to the Path LMS Reporting API, you will need to provide your bearer token as part of the Authorization header.

Sample Header:

--header 'Authorization: Bearer yourBearerToken'

Sample Bearer Token Entry in Postman

Sample Bearer Token Entry in Apollo Server

Note: If you are using Apollo Server for testing, please navigate to the Testing with Apollo Server section for more insight into adding your bearer token.

Testing

To facilitate the reporting API testing, we have created a dedicated Postman collection that can be accessed below. In addition to the Postman collection, we have also created an Apollo server instance that will allow you to test the reporting API.

Note: For testing purposes and to get a deeper understanding of the Reporting API capabilities, we recommend that you leverage Apollo Server for testing. The Apollo Server sandbox uses the GraphQL schema to generate the testing environment, this means that any changes that are made to the schema are instantly reflected on the Apollo Server testing environment.

The Path LMS development team has created a dedicated Postman collection as Postman is one of the most common API testing tools. While we will do our best to ensure that the collection is updated, the true source of truth will always be found through Apollo Server.

Testing with Postman

You can use the Run in Postman button below to import our Reporting API collection for testing.

When you click on the Run in Postman button you will be taken to a Postman webpage where you have the option of viewing the API collection, copying the API collection, or forking the API collection.

Note: We recommend that you fork the Postman collection. Forking the API collection will provide additional functionality for you. Notably, when you fork a collection, a relationship is created between the parent collection and your collection. Having this relationship allows you to update your collection if any changes are made to the parent collection. If you copy the collection you are not able to use the same functionality.

For additional insight into how forking a Postman collection works, please visit the Postman documentation.

Testing with Apollo Server

To explore and test the capabilities of the Reporting API, we have configured an Apollo Server environment.

  • Production

Navigating to the environment will allow you to interact with the Reporting API using the Apollo Server interface.

When you click on one of the link above you will be taken to a landing page where you can click on Query Your Server to interact with the Apollo server playground.

Before you can interact with the Apollo playground, you will need to enter your Bearer Token. Entering the bearer token will authenticate you against our servers. To enter your token, please click on the gear icon to the left of the Publish button.

Clicking on the gear icon will reveal the API settings. On this page you will need to add your bearer token under the Shared headers option. Please select Authorization as the header key and make sure that your value is defined as Bearer yourBearerToken. For example, if my bearer token is 123, I would define the value as Bearer 123.

Note: The bearer token will need to be generated using the API credentials that have been provided to you. For further insight on generating the bearer token, please visit the authentication section.

Query Variables

Overview

In an effort to improve the performance of the Reporting API and to allow you to define the type of data that you need, we have introduced variables that will need to be defined as part of your query request. You can find a full list of these variables below.

Variable Description Data Type Sample
startDate Start date to filter report. String "YYYY-MM-DD"
endDate End date to filter report. String "YYYY-MM-DD"
limit Limit the response to x number of records. int 50
offset Offset the records that are returned by x amount. int 0
withArchived Return archived records. boolean true
pastEvents Return past event records. boolean true
onlyHidden Return hidden items. boolean true
courseIds Return data for courses with the corresponding IDs. array ["123", "456"]
teamIds Return data for specified team IDs. array ["123", "456"]

Note: We encourage you to explore the type definitions listed below to get a better understand of when you will need to leverage these variables.

Using Query Variables

In the following example, when we want to query for user data, a couple of variables are exposed to us.

  • limit: Defines how many records are returned.
  • offset: Defines an offset value. For example, if you define offset as 50 and limit as 50, the response will return records 51-101.

Example:

Pre-Configured Reports

Overview

The purpose of this section is to provide some quick queries that you can use to retrieve Path LMS data via the Reporting API. Before you try these pre-configured reports, we recommend that you review the authentication and query variables sections as they have important information on making requests to the Reporting API.

We also encourage you to review the recipes section to make sure that you are using the Reporting API to it's fullest advantage.

Each of the sample queries that we have provided will contain a Try the Query button. When you click on this button, you will be redirected to Apollo Server where you can run the query. Before you click on this button, you should authenticate your Apollo Server to make sure that you are able to run the query. If the query contains variables you should also make sure that query has the appropriate variables defined.

Account Queries

The Reporting API returns data wrapped around two different objects. You are able to request data with respect to your account or with request to Path LMS Teams. If you request data with respect to your account, you will be able to retrieve site wide data. For example, all the users that exist on your account, all of your events, all of your presentations, etc...

Users Report

The Users Report query will allow you to retrieve both user data and general activity data for a given user. We will return basic information about the user as well as general information about the items that they have interacted with during the given time frame provided by the startDate and endDate variables.

Note: If you are an integrated client, it may be useful to also request the uid and username data fields as part of your request. You may find this information useful as you try to reference the records against your main database of record. Please make sure you include these in the users node.

To review the data that is available for your account, navigate to the account definitions by clicking here.

Available User Data:

{
  "id": "4",
  "email": "xyz789",
  "firstName": "xyz789",
  "lastLogin": "2007-12-03",
  "lastName": "abc123",
  "stateCode": "abc123",
  "teamId": 123,
  "timeZone": "xyz789",
  "registrationDate": "2007-12-03",
  "uid": "xyz789",
  "userId": 987,
  "accountId": 987,
  "admin": false,
  "assessmentPassedCount": 987,
  "assessmentFailedCount": 987,
  "completions": [Completion],
  "countryCode": "abc123",
  "certificatesEarnedCount": 987,
  "customAnswers": [SurveyQuestionAnswer],
  "firstLogin": "2007-12-03",
  "groups": [Group],
  "language": "xyz789",
  "lastActive": "2007-12-03",
  "itemViewCount": 123,
  "presentationsViewCount": 987,
  "createdAt": "2007-12-03",
  "creditTypesForCreditsCertified": [
    CreditTypeForCreditCertified
  ],
  "certificatesCount": 987,
  "username": "xyz789",
  "creditTypesForCompletedValues": [
    CreditTypeForCompletedValue
  ],
  "physician": true,
  "presentations": [Presentation]
}

Events Report

The Events Report query will allow you to retrieve event data for the events that exist on your account. Running this query will return both information about individual events as well as user analytics for the events. By default, we will only return data for events that are not archived within the given timeframe, however, you can modify the request by switching withArchived to true.

One key detail to note here is that when a live event is transitioned from a live event to an event recording it becomes a presentation. This request will also return presentation analytics.

Events Data:

{
  "id": 4,
  "archivedAt": "2007-12-03",
  "createdAt": "2007-12-03",
  "deletedAt": "2007-12-03",
  "endedOn": "2007-12-03",
  "name": "abc123",
  "presentationAmount": 987,
  "presentations": [EventPresentation],
  "startedOn": "2007-12-03",
  "status": "xyz789",
  "userVisitsCount": 123,
  "userVisitsDetail": [EventUser]
}

Event Presentation Data:

{
  "id": "4",
  "name": "xyz789",
  "parentEventSection": "abc123",
  "userVisits": [UserView],
  "userVisitsCount": 123,
  "totalVisitsCount": 987,
  "playCount": 123,
  "hoursWatched": 987.65,
  "status": "abc123"
}

Event User Data:

{
  "id": 4,
  "email": "abc123",
  "firstName": "xyz789",
  "lastLogin": "2007-12-03",
  "lastName": "abc123",
  "stateCode": "abc123",
  "timeZone": "xyz789",
  "registrationDate": "2007-12-03",
  "uid": "abc123",
  "userId": 123,
  "eventItemViews": 123
}

Courses Report

The Courses Report will return high-level information about the courses on your Path site. The report will return high-level information about a course as a whole and high-level information about the course items within the course. At the course item level, we are also able to return credits that are linked to the individual course items.

Available Course Data:

{
  "id": "4",
  "accountId": 4,
  "averageRating": 123.45,
  "courseItems": [CourseItem],
  "createdAt": "2007-12-03",
  "deletedAt": "2007-12-03",
  "endDate": "2007-12-03",
  "courseItemViewCount": 987,
  "userVisitsCount": 987,
  "courseItemViews": [CourseItemView],
  "name": "xyz789",
  "ratingsCount": 987,
  "startDate": "2007-12-03",
  "status": "abc123",
  "featured": true,
  "teamId": 123
}

Available Course Items Views Data:

{
  "name": "xyz789",
  "type": "abc123",
  "createdAt": "2007-12-03",
  "userVisitsCount": 123,
  "credits": [CreditsAssignedValues]
}

Course Presentations Report

The Course Presentations Report will return high-level information about the presentations on your Path LMS site. In addition to returning high-level information about the presentations, we will also return user visit data for the given course. The user visits data will include information about the user as well as the visits that they have contributed to the corresponding presentation.

Available Course Presentations Data:

{
  "id": 4,
  "accountId": 987,
  "teamId": 123,
  "courseId": 123,
  "name": "xyz789",
  "parentCourseSection": "xyz789",
  "userVisits": [UserView],
  "userVisitsCount": 987,
  "totalVisitsCount": 987,
  "playCount": 123,
  "hoursWatched": 987.65,
  "credits": "abc123",
  "status": "xyz789"
}

Available User Visits Data:

{
  "id": 4,
  "email": "abc123",
  "firstName": "xyz789",
  "lastLogin": "2007-12-03",
  "lastName": "abc123",
  "stateCode": "xyz789",
  "teamId": 123,
  "timeZone": "abc123",
  "registrationDate": "2007-12-03",
  "uid": "abc123",
  "userId": 123,
  "firstLogin": "2007-12-03",
  "itemViewCount": 987,
  "videoVisits": [VideoVisit],
  "completedAt": "2007-12-03"
}

Certified Credits Report

The Certified Credits Report allows you to retrieve high-level data on the credits that have been earned for the users on your Path LMS site. This query will also return information about the certificates that have been earned by each user.

Note: The certified credits report will only return credits that have been earned by a user through a certificate of completion. For example, if you have setup a course with a presentation (1.0 credit), a survey (0.75 credits), and a certificate of completion, the credits will only appear in the report if the user has completed both the presentation and the survey to generate the certificate of completion.

Available Certified Credits Report Data:

{
  "id": 4,
  "email": "abc123",
  "firstName": "xyz789",
  "lastLogin": "2007-12-03",
  "lastName": "xyz789",
  "stateCode": "abc123",
  "teamId": 123,
  "timeZone": "abc123",
  "registrationDate": "2007-12-03",
  "uid": "abc123",
  "userId": 987,
  "creditTypesForCreditsCertified": [
    CreditTypeForCreditCertified
  ],
  "certificatesCount": 123,
  "username": "xyz789",
  "freeCleUsed": 987.65,
  "certificates": [CertificateForUser]
}

Available Credit Types for Certified Credits Data:

{
  "id": 4,
  "name": "abc123",
  "active": false,
  "creditsCertifiedValues": 123.45,
  "creditsCertifiedDetails": [CreditsCertifiedValues]
}

Available Certificates Data:

{
  "id": "4",
  "name": "xyz789",
  "courseId": 987,
  "courseAndSection": "xyz789",
  "metadataValues": [MetadataValue],
  "awardedAt": "2007-12-03",
  "credits": "abc123"
}

Recipes

Overview

The purpose of this section is to guide you through leveraging the Reporting API to it's fullest potential. In this Recipes section we will be going over some common uses cases that you may find useful as you work with the Reporting API.

Requesting Data Formatted as a CSV File

Integrating the Reporting API

Pulling External or Custom Data Fields Into Your Report

If you are a client that using one of our integrations, it is not uncommon for you to store additional user details from your AMS/CRM directly on Path LMS. For example, you may want to pull a user's license number, profession, or professional title from your AMS/CRM system and store that on Path LMS so that you can display these fields when a user earns a certificate of completion. Path LMS stores this data as custom registration questions on the database. This data is stored as a key value pair, for example, you may have created a custom registration question User License Number that can store this data.

You may want to incorporate this data into your reports to get additional insight into the types of users that are purchasing content on your LMS site. For example, you may see that Physicians are the biggest purchasing demographic on your LMS site.

customAnswers {
  questionText
  answerText
}

We have exposed this data in a few of the queries.

  • Sales By Bundle
  • Users
  • User Sales

You could run the following query:

Variables:

{
  "startDate": "2023-01-01",
  "endDate": "2023-05-01",
  "offset": 0,
  "limit": 50
}

Query:

query Account(: Date!, : Date!, : Int, : Int) {
  account {
    id
    name
    slug
    salesByUserReport(startDate: , endDate: , offset: , limit: ) {
      id
      email
      firstName
      lastName
      uid
      customAnswers {
        answerText
        questionText
      }
      totalRefunded
      revenue
      ordersCount
    }
  }
}

This particular request would return high-level information about orders that were made by an individual user. Notably, we would return the following user information:

  • id
  • email
  • firstName
  • lastName
  • uid
  • customAnswers (data saved from your AMS/CRM system)

Manipulating Your Query

Unlike RESTful APIs that return a set amount of data, GraphQL-based APIs allow you to define the data fields that you are interested in. If we take a look at the Users query, we can see that we have quite a bit of information available:

{
  "id": "4",
  "email": "xyz789",
  "firstName": "xyz789",
  "lastLogin": "2007-12-03",
  "lastName": "xyz789",
  "stateCode": "xyz789",
  "timeZone": "xyz789",
  "registrationDate": "2007-12-03",
  "uid": "xyz789",
  "admin": true,
  "assessmentPassedCount": 987,
  "assessmentFailedCount": 123,
  "completions": [Completion],
  "countryCode": "xyz789",
  "certificatesEarnedCount": 123,
  "customAnswers": [SurveyQuestionAnswer],
  "firstLogin": "2007-12-03",
  "groups": [Group],
  "language": "xyz789",
  "lastActive": "2007-12-03",
  "itemViewCount": 987,
  "presentationsViewCount": 123,
  "createdAt": "2007-12-03",
  "creditTypesForCreditsCertified": [
    CreditTypeForCreditCertified
  ],
  "certificatesCount": 123,
  "username": "xyz789",
  "creditTypesForCompletedValues": [
    CreditTypeForCompletedValue
  ],
  "physician": false,
  "presentations": [Presentation]
}

Although there are many data fields available, there are some that you may not find applicable to you or relevant to your reporting needs. If you don't need these data fields, you can simply remove them from your request and GraphQL will omit them from the response data that is returned to you.

Sample Adjusted Query:

query Users (: Int, : Int) {
  account {
    users(offset: , limit: ) {
      id
      email
      firstName
      lastName
      lastLogin
      uid
      username
      createdAt
      lastActive
      firstLogin
    }
  }
}

Crafting a GraphQL Query

Query:

query Query(: Int, : Int) {
  account {
    id
    name
    users(offset: , limit: ) {
      firstName
      lastName
      email
      username
      firstLogin
      createdAt
      lastLogin
      lastActive
    }
  }
}
Contact

PathLMS Admin Support

[email protected]

API Endpoints
# Production:
https://data-api.pathlms.com/graphql

Queries

account

Description

This query will allow you access to account data. Users can call this query with a valid authorization access token. All queries under this top level query will report data with account data.

Response

Returns an Account

Example

Query
query account {
  account {
    details {
      ...BaseAccountFragment
    }
    assessmentsReport {
      ...AssessmentFragment
    }
    assignmentsReport {
      ...AssignmentFragment
    }
    certificatesReport {
      ...CertificateFragment
    }
    certifiedCreditsReport {
      ...CertifiedCreditFragment
    }
    courses {
      ...CourseFragment
    }
    coursePresentations {
      ...CoursePresentationFragment
    }
    creditsCompletedReport {
      ...CreditsCompletedUserFragment
    }
    events {
      ...EventFragment
    }
    inPersonEventsReport {
      ...InPersonEventFragment
    }
    liveWebEventsReport {
      ...WebinarFragment
    }
    scormReport {
      ...ScormFragment
    }
    surveysReport {
      ...SurveyFragment
    }
    users {
      ...UserFragment
    }
    assessmentsTotalCount
    assignmentsTotalCount
    certificatesTotalCount
    certifiedCreditsTotalCount
    coursesTotalCount
    coursePresentationsTotalCount
    eventsTotalCount
    inPersonEventsTotalCount
    liveWebEventsTotalCount
    scormTotalCount
    surveysTotalCount
    usersTotalCount
    commerceSalesByContentTotalCount
    couponsTotalCount
    discountsTotalCount
    externalActivityTotalCount
    ordersTotalCount
    orderItemsTotalCount
    refundsDetailsTotalCount
    refundsTotalCount
    salesByCategoryTotalCount
    salesByUserTotalCount
    salesByBundleTotalCount
    coupons {
      ...CouponFragment
    }
    discounts {
      ...DiscountFragment
    }
    externalActivityReport {
      ...ExternalActivityFragment
    }
    ordersReport {
      ...OrderFragment
    }
    orderItemsReport {
      ...OrderItemFragment
    }
    productCatalogReport {
      ...ProductCatalogFragment
    }
    refundsDetailsReport {
      ...RefundOrderItemFragment
    }
    refundsReport {
      ...RefundOrderFragment
    }
    salesByCategoryReport {
      ...SaleByCategoryFragment
    }
    salesByUserReport {
      ...UserSaleFragment
    }
    commerceSalesByContentReport {
      ...ContentSaleFragment
    }
    commerceSalesByContentDetailsReport {
      ...ContentSaleOrderItemFragment
    }
    userCount
    salesByBundleReport {
      ...SaleByBundleFragment
    }
  }
}
Response
{
  "data": {
    "account": {
      "details": BaseAccount,
      "assessmentsReport": [Assessment],
      "assignmentsReport": [Assignment],
      "certificatesReport": [Certificate],
      "certifiedCreditsReport": [CertifiedCredit],
      "courses": [Course],
      "coursePresentations": [CoursePresentation],
      "creditsCompletedReport": [CreditsCompletedUser],
      "events": [Event],
      "inPersonEventsReport": [InPersonEvent],
      "liveWebEventsReport": [Webinar],
      "scormReport": [Scorm],
      "surveysReport": [Survey],
      "users": [User],
      "assessmentsTotalCount": 123,
      "assignmentsTotalCount": 123,
      "certificatesTotalCount": 123,
      "certifiedCreditsTotalCount": 987,
      "coursesTotalCount": 123,
      "coursePresentationsTotalCount": 123,
      "eventsTotalCount": 987,
      "inPersonEventsTotalCount": 987,
      "liveWebEventsTotalCount": 123,
      "scormTotalCount": 987,
      "surveysTotalCount": 123,
      "usersTotalCount": 987,
      "commerceSalesByContentTotalCount": 987,
      "couponsTotalCount": 123,
      "discountsTotalCount": 123,
      "externalActivityTotalCount": 987,
      "ordersTotalCount": 123,
      "orderItemsTotalCount": 123,
      "refundsDetailsTotalCount": 987,
      "refundsTotalCount": 987,
      "salesByCategoryTotalCount": 123,
      "salesByUserTotalCount": 987,
      "salesByBundleTotalCount": 123,
      "coupons": [Coupon],
      "discounts": [Discount],
      "externalActivityReport": [ExternalActivity],
      "ordersReport": [Order],
      "orderItemsReport": [OrderItem],
      "productCatalogReport": [ProductCatalog],
      "refundsDetailsReport": [RefundOrderItem],
      "refundsReport": [RefundOrder],
      "salesByCategoryReport": [SaleByCategory],
      "salesByUserReport": [UserSale],
      "commerceSalesByContentReport": [ContentSale],
      "commerceSalesByContentDetailsReport": [
        ContentSaleOrderItem
      ],
      "userCount": 987,
      "salesByBundleReport": [SaleByBundle]
    }
  }
}
Queries

courseItemViewsReport

Response

Returns [CourseItemViewReport]!

Arguments
Name Description
courseIds - [Int!]!
offset - Int
limit - Int

Example

Query
query courseItemViewsReport(
  $courseIds: [Int!]!,
  $offset: Int,
  $limit: Int
) {
  courseItemViewsReport(
    courseIds: $courseIds,
    offset: $offset,
    limit: $limit
  ) {
    courseId
    itemViews {
      ...CourseItemViewFragment
    }
  }
}
Variables
{"courseIds": [987], "offset": 123, "limit": 987}
Response
{
  "data": {
    "courseItemViewsReport": [
      {
        "courseId": "4",
        "itemViews": [CourseItemView]
      }
    ]
  }
}
Queries

courseUserVisits

Description

This query will get back course user visits data for specfied courses.

Response

Returns [CourseUserVisits]

Arguments
Name Description
courseIds - [Int!]
limit - Int
offset - Int

Example

Query
query courseUserVisits(
  $courseIds: [Int!],
  $limit: Int,
  $offset: Int
) {
  courseUserVisits(
    courseIds: $courseIds,
    limit: $limit,
    offset: $offset
  ) {
    id
    courseId
    details {
      ...CourseUserFragment
    }
  }
}
Variables
{"courseIds": [987], "limit": 123, "offset": 987}
Response
{
  "data": {
    "courseUserVisits": [
      {"id": 4, "courseId": 987, "details": [CourseUser]}
    ]
  }
}
Queries

liveWebEventArchiveViewersReport

Response

Returns [WebinarArchiveViewerReport]!

Arguments
Name Description
webinarIds - [Int!]!
offset - Int
limit - Int
startDate - Date!
endDate - Date!

Example

Query
query liveWebEventArchiveViewersReport(
  $webinarIds: [Int!]!,
  $offset: Int,
  $limit: Int,
  $startDate: Date!,
  $endDate: Date!
) {
  liveWebEventArchiveViewersReport(
    webinarIds: $webinarIds,
    offset: $offset,
    limit: $limit,
    startDate: $startDate,
    endDate: $endDate
  ) {
    webinarId
    userViews {
      ...WebinarArchiveViewerUserFragment
    }
  }
}
Variables
{
  "webinarIds": [123],
  "offset": 987,
  "limit": 123,
  "startDate": "2007-12-03",
  "endDate": "2007-12-03"
}
Response
{
  "data": {
    "liveWebEventArchiveViewersReport": [
      {
        "webinarId": 4,
        "userViews": [WebinarArchiveViewerUser]
      }
    ]
  }
}
Queries

liveWebEventAttendeesReport

Response

Returns [WebinarLiveAttendeeReport]!

Arguments
Name Description
users - [Int!]
uid - String
webinarIds - [Int!]
startDate - Date
endDate - Date
offset - Int
limit - Int

Example

Query
query liveWebEventAttendeesReport(
  $users: [Int!],
  $uid: String,
  $webinarIds: [Int!],
  $startDate: Date,
  $endDate: Date,
  $offset: Int,
  $limit: Int
) {
  liveWebEventAttendeesReport(
    users: $users,
    uid: $uid,
    webinarIds: $webinarIds,
    startDate: $startDate,
    endDate: $endDate,
    offset: $offset,
    limit: $limit
  ) {
    webinarId
    webinarName
    webinarParentType
    webinarParentId
    courseItemId
    startDate
    requiredAttendanceDuration
    actualEndTime
    externalCreditId
    attendees {
      ...WebinarAttendeeFragment
    }
  }
}
Variables
{
  "users": [123],
  "uid": "abc123",
  "webinarIds": [123],
  "startDate": "2007-12-03",
  "endDate": "2007-12-03",
  "offset": 987,
  "limit": 123
}
Response
{
  "data": {
    "liveWebEventAttendeesReport": [
      {
        "webinarId": "4",
        "webinarName": "abc123",
        "webinarParentType": "xyz789",
        "webinarParentId": 4,
        "courseItemId": 4,
        "startDate": "2007-12-03",
        "requiredAttendanceDuration": 987,
        "actualEndTime": "2007-12-03",
        "externalCreditId": "abc123",
        "attendees": [WebinarAttendee]
      }
    ]
  }
}

Queries

liveWebEventRegistrationsReport

Response

Returns [WebinarRegistrationReport]!

Arguments
Name Description
users - [Int!]
uid - String
webinarIds - [Int!]
startDate - Date
endDate - Date
offset - Int
limit - Int

Example

Query
query liveWebEventRegistrationsReport(
  $users: [Int!],
  $uid: String,
  $webinarIds: [Int!],
  $startDate: Date,
  $endDate: Date,
  $offset: Int,
  $limit: Int
) {
  liveWebEventRegistrationsReport(
    users: $users,
    uid: $uid,
    webinarIds: $webinarIds,
    startDate: $startDate,
    endDate: $endDate,
    offset: $offset,
    limit: $limit
  ) {
    webinarId
    webinarName
    webinarParentType
    webinarParentId
    courseItemId
    startDate
    requiredAttendanceDuration
    actualEndTime
    externalCreditId
    registrations {
      ...WebinarAttendeeFragment
    }
  }
}
Variables
{
  "users": [987],
  "uid": "abc123",
  "webinarIds": [987],
  "startDate": "2007-12-03",
  "endDate": "2007-12-03",
  "offset": 123,
  "limit": 987
}
Response
{

      {
        "webinarId": 4,
        "webinarName": "xyz789",
        "webinarParentType": "xyz789",
        "webinarParentId": 4,
        "courseItemId": "4",
        "startDate": "2007-12-03",
        "requiredAttendanceDuration": 987,
        "actualEndTime": "2007-12-03",
        "externalCreditId": "xyz789",
        "registrations": [WebinarAttendee]
      }
    ]
  }
}
Queries

metadata

Description

This query will get back metadata which can be filtered by ids, metadataTemplateIds, and courseIds

Response

Returns [Metadata]

Arguments
Name Description
ids - [Int]
metadataTemplateIds - [Int]
courseIds - [Int]
limit - Int
offset - Int

Example

Query
query metadata(
  $ids: [Int],
  $metadataTemplateIds: [Int],
  $courseIds: [Int],
  $limit: Int,
  $offset: Int
) {
  metadata(
    ids: $ids,
    metadataTemplateIds: $metadataTemplateIds,
    courseIds: $courseIds,
    limit: $limit,
    offset: $offset
  ) {
    id
    metadatableType
    metadatableId
    metadataTemplate {
      ...MetadataTemplateFragment
    }
    key
    value
    valueString
    payload
    payloadString
    createdAt
    updatedAt
  }
}
Variables
{
  "ids": [123],
  "metadataTemplateIds": [123],
  "courseIds": [123],
  "limit": 987,
  "offset": 987
}
Response
{
  "data": {
    "metadata": [
      {
        "id": 4,
        "metadatableType": "abc123",
        "metadatableId": 987,
        "metadataTemplate": MetadataTemplate,
        "key": "xyz789",
        "value": {},
        "valueString": "xyz789",
        "payload": {},
        "payloadString": "abc123",
        "createdAt": "2007-12-03",
        "updatedAt": "2007-12-03"
      }
    ]
  }
}
Queries

metadataTemplates

Description

This query will get back metadata templates (supports both templates and metadata pagination)

Response

Returns [MetadataTemplate]

Arguments
Name Description
limit - Int
offset - Int
metadataLimit - Int
metadataOffset - Int

Example

Query
query metadataTemplates(
  $limit: Int,
  $offset: Int,
  $metadataLimit: Int,
  $metadataOffset: Int
) {
  metadataTemplates(
    limit: $limit,
    offset: $offset,
    metadataLimit: $metadataLimit,
    metadataOffset: $metadataOffset
  ) {
    id
    accountId
    valueType
    key
    objectKey
    timeZone
    external
    allowNullValues
    createdAt
    updatedAt
    metadata {
      ...MetadataFragment
    }
  }
}
Variables
{"limit": 123, "offset": 123, "metadataLimit": 123, "metadataOffset": 987}
Response
{
  "data": {
    "metadataTemplates": [
      {
        "id": "4",
        "accountId": "4",
        "valueType": "xyz789",
        "key": "xyz789",
        "objectKey": "xyz789",
        "timeZone": "xyz789",
        "external": true,
        "allowNullValues": true,
        "createdAt": "2007-12-03",
        "updatedAt": "2007-12-03",
        "metadata": [Metadata]
      }
    ]
  }
}
Queries

root

Response

Returns a String

Example

Query
query root {
  root
}
Response
{"data": {"root": "abc123"}}
Queries

teams

Description

This query will allow you access to a team's data. Users can hit this query with a valid authorization access token. All queries under this top level query will report data with team data.

Response

Returns [Team]!

Arguments
Name Description
teamIds - [Int!]

Example

Query
query teams($teamIds: [Int!]) {
  teams(teamIds: $teamIds) {
    id
    name
    assessmentsReport {
      ...AssessmentFragment
    }
    assignmentsReport {
      ...AssignmentFragment
    }
    certificatesReport {
      ...CertificateFragment
    }
    certifiedCreditsReport {
      ...CertifiedCreditFragment
    }
    courses {
      ...CourseFragment
    }
    coursePresentations {
      ...CoursePresentationFragment
    }
    creditsCompletedReport {
      ...CreditsCompletedUserFragment
    }
    events {
      ...EventFragment
    }
    inPersonEventsReport {
      ...InPersonEventFragment
    }
    liveWebEventsReport {
      ...WebinarFragment
    }
    scormReport {
      ...ScormFragment
    }
    surveysReport {
      ...SurveyFragment
    }
    users {
      ...UserFragment
    }
    assessmentsTotalCount
    assignmentsTotalCount
    certificatesTotalCount
    certifiedCreditsTotalCount
    coursesTotalCount
    coursePresentationsTotalCount
    eventsTotalCount
    inPersonEventsTotalCount
    liveWebEventsTotalCount
    scormTotalCount
    surveysTotalCount
    usersTotalCount
    account {
      ...BaseAccountFragment
    }
    creditsCompletedTotalCount
  }
}
Variables
{"teamIds": [123]}
Response
{
  "data": {
    "teams": [
      {
        "id": "4",
        "name": "xyz789",
        "assessmentsReport": [Assessment],
        "assignmentsReport": [Assignment],
        "certificatesReport": [Certificate],
        "certifiedCreditsReport": [CertifiedCredit],
        "courses": [Course],
        "coursePresentations": [CoursePresentation],
        "creditsCompletedReport": [CreditsCompletedUser],
        "events": [Event],
        "inPersonEventsReport": [InPersonEvent],
        "liveWebEventsReport": [Webinar],
        "scormReport": [Scorm],
        "surveysReport": [Survey],
        "users": [User],
        "assessmentsTotalCount": 987,
        "assignmentsTotalCount": 123,
        "certificatesTotalCount": 123,
        "certifiedCreditsTotalCount": 123,
        "coursesTotalCount": 987,
        "coursePresentationsTotalCount": 123,
        "eventsTotalCount": 123,
        "inPersonEventsTotalCount": 123,
        "liveWebEventsTotalCount": 987,
        "scormTotalCount": 987,
        "surveysTotalCount": 123,
        "usersTotalCount": 123,
        "account": BaseAccount,
        "creditsCompletedTotalCount": 123
      }
    ]
  }
}
Queries

teamsList

Description

This query will give you a list of team Ids and team names. Users can hit this query with a valid authorization access token and find the team ids they would like to use in the reports.

Response

Returns [BaseTeam]!

Example

Query
query teamsList {
  teamsList {
    id
    name
  }
}
Response
{
  "data": {
    "teamsList": [
      {
        "id": "4",
        "name": "abc123"
      }
    ]
  }
}
Queries

userItemWithVisitsReport

Response

Returns [UserItemVisits!]!

Arguments
Name Description
userIds - [ID!]
uids - [String]
startDate - Date!
endDate - Date!
visitsStartDate - Date
visitsEndDate - Date
withArchived - Boolean Default = false
offset - Int Default = 0
limit - Int Default = 50

Example

Query
query userItemWithVisitsReport(
  $userIds: [ID!],
  $uids: [String],
  $startDate: Date!,
  $endDate: Date!,
  $visitsStartDate: Date,
  $visitsEndDate: Date,
  $withArchived: Boolean,
  $offset: Int,
  $limit: Int
) {
  userItemWithVisitsReport(
    userIds: $userIds,
    uids: $uids,
    startDate: $startDate,
    endDate: $endDate,
    visitsStartDate: $visitsStartDate,
    visitsEndDate: $visitsEndDate,
    withArchived: $withArchived,
    offset: $offset,
    limit: $limit
  ) {
    userId
    uid
    userKey
    userItems {
      ...UserItemFragment
    }
    archivedAt
    firstName
    lastName
    email
    username
    language
    countryCode
    stateCode
    timeZone
    groups
  }
}
Variables
{
  "userIds": [4],
  "uids": ["abc123"],
  "startDate": "2007-12-03",
  "endDate": "2007-12-03",
  "visitsStartDate": "2007-12-03",
  "visitsEndDate": "2007-12-03",
  "withArchived": false,
  "offset": 0,
  "limit": 50
}
Response
{
  "data": {
    "userItemWithVisitsReport": [
      {
        "userId": 4,
        "uid": "xyz789",
        "userKey": "xyz789",
        "userItems": [UserItem],
        "archivedAt": "2007-12-03",
        "firstName": "xyz789",
        "lastName": "xyz789",
        "email": "xyz789",
        "username": "abc123",
        "language": "xyz789",
        "countryCode": "abc123",
        "stateCode": "xyz789",
        "timeZone": "abc123",
        "groups": ["xyz789"]
      }
    ]
  }
}
Queries

userMetadata

Response

Returns [UserMetadata!]!

Arguments
Name Description
ids - [Int!]
userIds - [Int!]
userMetadataTemplateIds - [Int!]
keys - [String!]
offset - Int
limit - Int

Example

Query
query userMetadata(
  $ids: [Int!],
  $userIds: [Int!],
  $userMetadataTemplateIds: [Int!],
  $keys: [String!],
  $offset: Int,
  $limit: Int
) {
  userMetadata(
    ids: $ids,
    userIds: $userIds,
    userMetadataTemplateIds: $userMetadataTemplateIds,
    keys: $keys,
    offset: $offset,
    limit: $limit
  ) {
    id
    accountId
    userId
    user {
      ...UserFragment
    }
    userMetadataTemplateId
    userMetadataTemplate {
      ...UserMetadataTemplateFragment
    }
    key
    value
    parentMetadataId
    parentMetadata {
      ...UserMetadataFragment
    }
    childMetadata {
      ...UserMetadataFragment
    }
    createdAt
    updatedAt
    deletedAt
  }
}
Variables
{
  "ids": [987],
  "userIds": [123],
  "userMetadataTemplateIds": [123],
  "keys": ["abc123"],
  "offset": 123,
  "limit": 987
}
Response
{
  "data": {
    "userMetadata": [
      {
        "id": "4",
        "accountId": 4,
        "userId": 4,
        "user": User,
        "userMetadataTemplateId": 4,
        "userMetadataTemplate": UserMetadataTemplate,
        "key": "abc123",
        "value": {},
        "parentMetadataId": 4,
        "parentMetadata": UserMetadata,
        "childMetadata": [UserMetadata],
        "createdAt": "2007-12-03",
        "updatedAt": "2007-12-03",
        "deletedAt": "2007-12-03"
      }
    ]
  }
}
Queries

userMetadataTemplates

Response

Returns [UserMetadataTemplate!]!

Arguments
Name Description
ids - [Int!]
keys - [String!]
valueTypes - [String!]
offset - Int
limit - Int

Example

Query
query userMetadataTemplates(
  $ids: [Int!],
  $keys: [String!],
  $valueTypes: [String!],
  $offset: Int,
  $limit: Int
) {
  userMetadataTemplates(
    ids: $ids,
    keys: $keys,
    valueTypes: $valueTypes,
    offset: $offset,
    limit: $limit
  ) {
    id
    accountId
    key
    longKey
    question
    valueType
    timezone
    external
    allowNullValues
    allowUserEditResponse
    obfuscateSensitiveData
    userMetadataValidatorId
    userMetadata {
      ...UserMetadataFragment
    }
    createdAt
    updatedAt
  }
}
Variables
{
  "ids": [987],
  "keys": ["abc123"],
  "valueTypes": ["xyz789"],
  "offset": 987,
  "limit": 123
}
Response
{
  "data": {
    "userMetadataTemplates": [
      {
        "id": "4",
        "accountId": 4,
        "key": "abc123",
        "longKey": "abc123",
        "question": "xyz789",
        "valueType": "abc123",
        "timezone": "xyz789",
        "external": true,
        "allowNullValues": true,
        "allowUserEditResponse": false,
        "obfuscateSensitiveData": false,
        "userMetadataValidatorId": "4",
        "userMetadata": [UserMetadata],
        "createdAt": "2007-12-03",
        "updatedAt": "2007-12-03"
      }
    ]
  }
}
Queries

userPresentationsReport

Response

Returns [UserPresentationReport]!

Arguments
Name Description
userIds - [Int!]!
offset - Int
limit - Int

Example

Query
query userPresentationsReport(
  $userIds: [Int!]!,
  $offset: Int,
  $limit: Int
) {
  userPresentationsReport(
    userIds: $userIds,
    offset: $offset,
    limit: $limit
  ) {
    userId
    presentations {
      ...UserPresentationFragment
    }
  }
}
Variables
{"userIds": [987], "offset": 123, "limit": 123}
Response
{
  "data": {
    "userPresentationsReport": [
      {"userId": 4, "presentations": [UserPresentation]}
    ]
  }
}

Types

Account

Fields
Field Name Description
details - BaseAccount
assessmentsReport - [Assessment]! This report will give you a high level overview of Assessments within a Course
Arguments
withArchived - Boolean
startDate - Date!
endDate - Date!
offset - Int
limit - Int
assignmentsReport - [Assignment]! This report will give you a high level overview of Assignments within a Course.
Arguments
withArchived - Boolean
startDate - Date!
endDate - Date!
offset - Int
limit - Int
certificatesReport - [Certificate]! This report will give you a high level overview of Certificates within a Course.
Arguments
withArchived - Boolean
startDate - Date!
endDate - Date!
offset - Int
limit - Int
certifiedCreditsReport - [CertifiedCredit]! This report will give you a high level overview of credits which are tied to an earned certificate. Users who have earned credits which are tied to an earned certificate are included in this report.
Arguments
userIds - [Int]
uid - String
startDate - Date!
endDate - Date!
offset - Int
limit - Int
noPagination - String
courses - [Course]! This report displays the total number of courses available. Each row contains detailed information about the available courses.
Arguments
metadataTemplateId - Int
withArchived - Boolean
offset - Int
limit - Int
coursePresentations - [CoursePresentation]! This report includes content and user activity for presentations included within courses across your site
Arguments
withArchived - Boolean
startDate - Date!
endDate - Date!
offset - Int
limit - Int
creditsCompletedReport - [CreditsCompletedUser]! This report will give you a high level overview of credits earned for items completed, regardless of whether the user earned a certificate
Arguments
offset - Int
limit - Int
events - [Event]! This report will give you a high level overview of data for all On-demand Events on your account. Each row contains content, visit, and status information for each event.
Arguments
withArchived - Boolean
offset - Int
limit - Int
inPersonEventsReport - [InPersonEvent]! This report will give you a high level overview of all data pertaining to your in-person (or on-site) event objects in Path.
Arguments
withArchived - Boolean
pastEvents - Boolean
startDate - Date!
endDate - Date!
offset - Int
limit - Int
liveWebEventsReport - [Webinar]! This report will give you a high level overview of web events on Path
Arguments
withArchived - Boolean
pastEvents - Boolean
offset - Int
limit - Int
scormReport - [Scorm]! This report contains a list of all SCORM content and general user engagement metrics, such as date first viewed, date completed, and item visits
Arguments
withArchived - Boolean
offset - Int
limit - Int
surveysReport - [Survey]! This report will give you a high level overview of survey data within a course.
Arguments
withArchived - Boolean
startDate - Date!
endDate - Date!
offset - Int
limit - Int
users - [User]! This report will give you a high level overview of user information regarding work completed over the given start/end dates inputted.
Arguments
userIds - [Int]
uid - String
offset - Int
limit - Int
assessmentsTotalCount - Int!
Arguments
withArchived - Boolean
assignmentsTotalCount - Int!
Arguments
withArchived - Boolean
startDate - Date!
endDate - Date!
certificatesTotalCount - Int!
Arguments
withArchived - Boolean
startDate - Date!
endDate - Date!
certifiedCreditsTotalCount - Int!
Arguments
startDate - Date!
endDate - Date!
coursesTotalCount - Int!
Arguments
withArchived - Boolean
coursePresentationsTotalCount - Int!
Arguments
withArchived - Boolean
startDate - Date!
endDate - Date!
eventsTotalCount - Int!
Arguments
withArchived - Boolean
inPersonEventsTotalCount - Int!
Arguments
withArchived - Boolean
pastEvents - Boolean
liveWebEventsTotalCount - Int!
Arguments
withArchived - Boolean
pastEvents - Boolean
scormTotalCount - Int!
Arguments
withArchived - Boolean
surveysTotalCount - Int!
Arguments
withArchived - Boolean
usersTotalCount - Int!
commerceSalesByContentTotalCount - Int!
Arguments
withArchived - Boolean
startDate - Date!
endDate - Date!
couponsTotalCount - Int!
discountsTotalCount - Int!
externalActivityTotalCount - Int!
Arguments
startDate - Date!
endDate - Date!
ordersTotalCount - Int!
Arguments
startDate - Date!
endDate - Date!
orderItemsTotalCount - Int!
Arguments
startDate - Date!
endDate - Date!
refundsDetailsTotalCount - Int!
Arguments
startDate - Date!
endDate - Date!
refundsTotalCount - Int!
Arguments
startDate - Date!
endDate - Date!
salesByCategoryTotalCount - Int!
Arguments
onlyHidden - Boolean
startDate - Date!
endDate - Date!
salesByUserTotalCount - Int!
salesByBundleTotalCount - Int!
Arguments
startDate - Date!
endDate - Date!
coupons - [Coupon]! This query will return the list of all the coupons in the account that have not been deleted. It will use the offset and limit parameters to paginate the results. The default offset (page) is 0 and the default limit is 50. This limit cannot exceed 150.
Arguments
offset - Int
limit - Int
discounts - [Discount]! This query will return the list of all the discounts in the account that have not been deleted. It will use the offset and limit parameters to paginate the results. The default offset (page) is 0 and the default limit is 50. This limit cannot exceed 150. The difference between this query and the coupons query is the data that is returned in the fields under this top level report.
Arguments
offset - Int
limit - Int
externalActivityReport - [ExternalActivity]! This query will return the list of all the external activities in the account that have not been deleted, falls within the date range between the specified start and end date parameters. It will also take into account a list of permitted users based off of the permissions laid out in Path. It will use the offset and limit parameters to paginate the results. The default offset (page) is 0 and the default limit is 50. This limit cannot exceed 150.
Arguments
startDate - Date!
endDate - Date!
offset - Int
limit - Int
ordersReport - [Order]! This query will return a list of Orders in the account that have not been deleted, has been paid, was created within the date range between the specified start and end date parameters, or paid at between the specified start and end date parameters. It will use the offset and limit parameters to paginate the results. The default offset (page) is 0 and the default limit is 50. This limit cannot exceed 150. It will also take into account a list of permitted users based off of the permissions laid out in Path.
Arguments
offset - Int
limit - Int
startDate - Date!
endDate - Date!
orderItemsReport - [OrderItem]! This query will return a list of Order Items in the account that have not been deleted, has been paid, was created within the date range between the start and end date parameters. It will use the offset and limit parameters to paginate the results. The default offset (page) is 0 and the default limit is 50. This limit cannot exceed 150.
Arguments
offset - Int
limit - Int
startDate - Date!
endDate - Date!
productCatalogReport - [ProductCatalog]! This query will return every sellable item in Path that has an item ID for an account. This item is sellable and has not been deleted. This page is commonly used to find the IDs of products for integration purposes. It will use the offset and limit parameters to paginate the results. The default offset (page) is 0 and the default limit is 50. This limit cannot exceed 150.
Arguments
withArchived - Boolean
startDate - Date!
endDate - Date!
offset - Int
limit - Int
refundsDetailsReport - [RefundOrderItem]! This query will return a list of Order Items in the account that have not been deleted, was refunded within the date range between the start and end date parameters. It will use the offset and limit parameters to paginate the results. The default offset (page) is 0 and the default limit is 50. This limit cannot exceed 150.
Arguments
offset - Int
limit - Int
startDate - Date!
endDate - Date!
refundsReport - [RefundOrder]! This query will return a list of Orders in the account that have not been deleted, a Refund was created within the date range between the start and end date parameters. It will use the offset and limit parameters to paginate the results. The default offset (page) is 0 and the default limit is 50. This limit cannot exceed 150.
Arguments
offset - Int
limit - Int
startDate - Date!
endDate - Date!
salesByCategoryReport - [SaleByCategory]! This query will groups sales, orders and revenue by the category of items included in the purchase transaction. This uses order and order items within the date range between the start and end date parameters. It will use the offset and limit parameters to paginate the results. The default offset (page) is 0 and the default limit is 50. This limit cannot exceed 150.
Arguments
onlyHidden - Boolean
startDate - Date!
endDate - Date!
offset - Int
limit - Int
salesByUserReport - [UserSale]! This query groups orders, revenue, and credit balances by user. This report is useful for things like: understanding all-time revenue for individual users, viewing and exporting a list of all orders for an individual user or viewing credit usage within the range of the start and end date parameters. It will use the offset and limit parameters to paginate the results. The default offset (page) is 0 and the default limit is 50. This limit cannot exceed 150.
Arguments
startDate - Date!
endDate - Date!
offset - Int
limit - Int
commerceSalesByContentReport - [ContentSale] This query groups sales and revenue by order item (content purchased). This report will also include archived content if the withArchived parameter is set to true. It uses the order items that were created within the date range between the start and end date parameters. It will use the offset and limit parameters to paginate the results. The default offset (page) is 0 and the default limit is 50. This limit cannot exceed 150.
Arguments
withArchived - Boolean
startDate - Date!
endDate - Date!
offset - Int
limit - Int
commerceSalesByContentDetailsReport - [ContentSaleOrderItem] This query gets details for content purchased for an order item. It uses a course item type and id and gets orders that were created within the date range between the start and end date parameters. It will use the offset and limit parameters to paginate the results. The default offset (page) is 0 and the default limit is 50. This limit cannot exceed 150.
Arguments
courseItems - [String!]
startDate - Date!
endDate - Date!
offset - Int
limit - Int
userCount - Int! This query will return a total count of users for an account. This count will include users that have not been deleted.
salesByBundleReport - [SaleByBundle]! This query retrieves a list of Order Items. It filters the results based on a set of criteria, including the date range, user IDs, and product types. It will use the offset and limit parameters to paginate the results. The default offset (page) is 0 and the default limit is 50. This limit cannot exceed 150.
Arguments
withArchived - Boolean
offset - Int
limit - Int
startDate - Date!
endDate - Date!
Example
{
  "details": BaseAccount,
  "assessmentsReport": [Assessment],
  "assignmentsReport": [Assignment],
  "certificatesReport": [Certificate],
  "certifiedCreditsReport": [CertifiedCredit],
  "courses": [Course],
  "coursePresentations": [CoursePresentation],
  "creditsCompletedReport": [CreditsCompletedUser],
  "events": [Event],
  "inPersonEventsReport": [InPersonEvent],
  "liveWebEventsReport": [Webinar],
  "scormReport": [Scorm],
  "surveysReport": [Survey],
  "users": [User],
  "assessmentsTotalCount": 123,
  "assignmentsTotalCount": 987,
  "certificatesTotalCount": 987,
  "certifiedCreditsTotalCount": 987,
  "coursesTotalCount": 123,
  "coursePresentationsTotalCount": 123,
  "eventsTotalCount": 987,
  "inPersonEventsTotalCount": 987,
  "liveWebEventsTotalCount": 123,
  "scormTotalCount": 123,
  "surveysTotalCount": 123,
  "usersTotalCount": 987,
  "commerceSalesByContentTotalCount": 123,
  "couponsTotalCount": 123,
  "discountsTotalCount": 987,
  "externalActivityTotalCount": 123,
  "ordersTotalCount": 987,
  "orderItemsTotalCount": 123,
  "refundsDetailsTotalCount": 123,
  "refundsTotalCount": 123,
  "salesByCategoryTotalCount": 987,
  "salesByUserTotalCount": 123,
  "salesByBundleTotalCount": 987,
  "coupons": [Coupon],
  "discounts": [Discount],
  "externalActivityReport": [ExternalActivity],
  "ordersReport": [Order],
  "orderItemsReport": [OrderItem],
  "productCatalogReport": [ProductCatalog],
  "refundsDetailsReport": [RefundOrderItem],
  "refundsReport": [RefundOrder],
  "salesByCategoryReport": [SaleByCategory],
  "salesByUserReport": [UserSale],
  "commerceSalesByContentReport": [ContentSale],
  "commerceSalesByContentDetailsReport": [
    ContentSaleOrderItem
  ],
  "userCount": 123,
  "salesByBundleReport": [SaleByBundle]
}
Types

Assessment

Fields
Field Name Description
id - ID! The Assessment Id
accountId - Int! The Account Id of the account that owns the assessment
teamId - Int The associated team id if the team report is being ran
courseId - Int The course Id the assessment belongs to
courseItemId - Int The course item Id the assessment belongs to
parentCourseSection - String! The parent course section name
createdAt - Date! The date the assessment was created
archivedAt - Date The date the assessment was archived
deletedAt - Date The date the assessment was disabled
name - String! The assessment name
questionsCount - Int! The number of questions associated to the assessment
failedCount - Int! The number of failed questions within the startDate - endDate time range
Arguments
startDate - Date!
endDate - Date!
passedCount - Int! The number of passed questions within the startDate - endDate time range
Arguments
startDate - Date!
endDate - Date!
averageScore - Int! The average score of the assessment within the startDate - endDate time range
Arguments
startDate - Date!
endDate - Date!
userVisits - Int! The number of users that viewed the assessment within the startDate - endDate time range
Arguments
startDate - Date!
endDate - Date!
passingScore - Int! The score needed to pass the assessment
credits - String! The credits used to access the assessment
status - String! The status of the assessment (active or inactive)
assessmentQuestions - [AssessmentQuestion] The sub report with details of the assessment questions
Arguments
startDate - Date!
endDate - Date!
assessmentScores - [AssessmentScore] The sub report with details of the assessment scores
Arguments
startDate - Date!
endDate - Date!
Example
{
  "id": 4,
  "accountId": 987,
  "teamId": 123,
  "courseId": 123,
  "courseItemId": 123,
  "parentCourseSection": "abc123",
  "createdAt": "2007-12-03",
  "archivedAt": "2007-12-03",
  "deletedAt": "2007-12-03",
  "name": "abc123",
  "questionsCount": 123,
  "failedCount": 123,
  "passedCount": 123,
  "averageScore": 987,
  "userVisits": 987,
  "passingScore": 123,
  "credits": "xyz789",
  "status": "abc123",
  "assessmentQuestions": [AssessmentQuestion],
  "assessmentScores": [AssessmentScore]
}
Types

AssessmentQuestion

Fields
Field Name Description
question - String! The question name
questionPool - String! The question pool name
correctPercent - Int! The percentage of correctly answered questions within the startDate - endDate range
Arguments
startDate - Date!
endDate - Date!
submissionCount - Int! The number of subbmited questions within the startDate - endDate range
Arguments
startDate - Date!
endDate - Date!
Example
{
  "question": "xyz789",
  "questionPool": "xyz789",
  "correctPercent": 123,
  "submissionCount": 987
}
Types

AssessmentScore

Fields
Field Name Description
id - Int! The assessment score Id
userId - Int! The userId field is the same as id, but easier to utilize for cross referencing in other systems
email - String! A users email as inputted in Path
username - String
firstName - String! A users first name as inputted in Path
lastName - String! A users last name as inputted in Path
language - String! The designated language for translations and text rendering in Path for a user.
countryCode - String The country a user designated in their Path profile
stateCode - String The state a user designated in their Path user profile
timeZone - String The timezone a user is in and is selected on the Path user profile.
firstLogin - Date When the user first signed into their Path account. Activity dates and timestamps are shown in Pacific Time (US & Canada)
lastLogin - Date When the user last signed into their Path account. Activity dates and timestamps are shown in Pacific Time (US & Canada)
lastActive - Date When the user was last signed into their Path account. Activity dates and timestamps are shown in Pacific Time (US & Canada)
groups - [Group] This is a list of groups a user is designated to in on an account.
uid - String
userKey - String
customRegistrationQuestions - [SurveyQuestionAnswer]! If an account has custom login questions configured, these records are useful to show a users answers during the login process.
score - String! The assessment score
submittedOn - Date! The submission date
spentTime - Date The time spent on the submission
status - String! The status of the submission (either passed or fail)
userAssessmentSubmissions - [AssessmentSubmission]! The submissions made by the user
Example
{
  "id": 123,
  "userId": 123,
  "email": "abc123",
  "username": "abc123",
  "firstName": "xyz789",
  "lastName": "xyz789",
  "language": "xyz789",
  "countryCode": "xyz789",
  "stateCode": "xyz789",
  "timeZone": "abc123",
  "firstLogin": "2007-12-03",
  "lastLogin": "2007-12-03",
  "lastActive": "2007-12-03",
  "groups": [Group],
  "uid": "abc123",
  "userKey": "xyz789",
  "customRegistrationQuestions": [SurveyQuestionAnswer],
  "score": "abc123",
  "submittedOn": "2007-12-03",
  "spentTime": "2007-12-03",
  "status": "xyz789",
  "userAssessmentSubmissions": [AssessmentSubmission]
}
Types

AssessmentSubmission

Fields
Field Name Description
question - String!
type - String!
correct - Boolean!
answer - String!
Example
{
  "question": "xyz789",
  "type": "xyz789",
  "correct": false,
  "answer": "abc123"
}
Types

Assignment

Fields
Field Name Description
id - Int! The Assignment Id
accountId - Int! The Account Id of the account that owns the Assignment
teamId - Int The associated team id if the team report is being ran
name - String! The Assignment name
courseId - Int The Course Id the Assignment belongs to
courseAndSection - String! The parent Course Section the Assignment belongs to
createdAt - Date! The date the Assignment was created
totalSubmissions - Int! The number of total submissions the Assignment has within the startDate - endDate time range
Arguments
startDate - Date!
endDate - Date!
passedCount - Int! The number of passed submissions the Assignment has within the startDate - endDate time range
Arguments
startDate - Date!
endDate - Date!
failedCount - Int! The number of failed submissions the Assignment has within the startDate - endDate time range
Arguments
startDate - Date!
endDate - Date!
userVisitsCount - Int! The number of user visits the Assignment has within the startDate - endDate time range
Arguments
startDate - Date!
endDate - Date!
submissions - [AssignmentSubmission!]! The sub report of submissions the Assignment has within the startDate - endDate time range
Arguments
startDate - Date!
endDate - Date!
passedSubmissions - [AssignmentSubmission!]! The sub report of passed submissions the Assignment has within the startDate - endDate time range
Arguments
startDate - Date!
endDate - Date!
failedSubmissions - [AssignmentSubmission!]! The sub report of failed submissions the Assignment has within the startDate - endDate time range
Arguments
startDate - Date!
endDate - Date!
credits - String! The credits used to access the Assignment
status - String! The Assignment status (active/inactive)
userVisits - [AssignmentUserView!]! The sub report of user visits the Assignment has within the startDate - endDate time range
Arguments
startDate - Date!
endDate - Date!
Example
{
  "id": 987,
  "accountId": 987,
  "teamId": 987,
  "name": "abc123",
  "courseId": 123,
  "courseAndSection": "xyz789",
  "createdAt": "2007-12-03",
  "totalSubmissions": 123,
  "passedCount": 987,
  "failedCount": 987,
  "userVisitsCount": 123,
  "submissions": [AssignmentSubmission],
  "passedSubmissions": [AssignmentSubmission],
  "failedSubmissions": [AssignmentSubmission],
  "credits": "xyz789",
  "status": "xyz789",
  "userVisits": [AssignmentUserView]
}
Types

AssignmentSubmission

Fields
Field Name Description
id - ID! A users database record id
userId - Int! The userId field is the same as id, but easier to utilize for cross referencing in other systems
email - String! A users email as inputted in Path
username - String
firstName - String! A users first name as inputted in Path
lastName - String! A users last name as inputted in Path
language - String! The designated language for translations and text rendering in Path for a user.
countryCode - String The country a user designated in their Path profile
stateCode - String The state a user designated in their Path user profile
timeZone - String The timezone a user is in and is selected on the Path user profile.
firstLogin - Date When the user first signed into their Path account. Activity dates and timestamps are shown in Pacific Time (US & Canada)
lastLogin - Date When the user last signed into their Path account. Activity dates and timestamps are shown in Pacific Time (US & Canada)
lastActive - Date When the user was last signed into their Path account. Activity dates and timestamps are shown in Pacific Time (US & Canada)
groups - [Group] This is a list of groups a user is designated to in on an account.
uid - String
userKey - String
customRegistrationQuestions - [SurveyQuestionAnswer]! If an account has custom login questions configured, these records are useful to show a users answers during the login process.
registrationDate - Date The date a user created an account in Path, accepted a registration link or was imported into Path
teamId - Int The associated team Id if the user is a team member
state - String!
submittedDate - Date!
gradedDate - Date!
instructorEmail - String!
Example
{
  "id": "4",
  "userId": 987,
  "email": "abc123",
  "username": "xyz789",
  "firstName": "xyz789",
  "lastName": "xyz789",
  "language": "abc123",
  "countryCode": "xyz789",
  "stateCode": "abc123",
  "timeZone": "abc123",
  "firstLogin": "2007-12-03",
  "lastLogin": "2007-12-03",
  "lastActive": "2007-12-03",
  "groups": [Group],
  "uid": "xyz789",
  "userKey": "xyz789",
  "customRegistrationQuestions": [SurveyQuestionAnswer],
  "registrationDate": "2007-12-03",
  "teamId": 123,
  "state": "abc123",
  "submittedDate": "2007-12-03",
  "gradedDate": "2007-12-03",
  "instructorEmail": "xyz789"
}
Types

AssignmentUserView

Fields
Field Name Description
id - ID! A users database record id
userId - Int! The userId field is the same as id, but easier to utilize for cross referencing in other systems
email - String! A users email as inputted in Path
username - String
firstName - String! A users first name as inputted in Path
lastName - String! A users last name as inputted in Path
language - String! The designated language for translations and text rendering in Path for a user.
countryCode - String The country a user designated in their Path profile
stateCode - String The state a user designated in their Path user profile
timeZone - String The timezone a user is in and is selected on the Path user profile.
firstLogin - Date When the user first signed into their Path account. Activity dates and timestamps are shown in Pacific Time (US & Canada)
lastLogin - Date When the user last signed into their Path account. Activity dates and timestamps are shown in Pacific Time (US & Canada)
lastActive - Date When the user was last signed into their Path account. Activity dates and timestamps are shown in Pacific Time (US & Canada)
groups - [Group] This is a list of groups a user is designated to in on an account.
uid - String
userKey - String
customRegistrationQuestions - [SurveyQuestionAnswer]! If an account has custom login questions configured, these records are useful to show a users answers during the login process.
registrationDate - Date The date a user created an account in Path, accepted a registration link or was imported into Path
teamId - Int The associated team Id if the user is a team member
visitedAt - Date!
Example
{
  "id": 4,
  "userId": 123,
  "email": "xyz789",
  "username": "abc123",
  "firstName": "abc123",
  "lastName": "xyz789",
  "language": "xyz789",
  "countryCode": "abc123",
  "stateCode": "abc123",
  "timeZone": "xyz789",
  "firstLogin": "2007-12-03",
  "lastLogin": "2007-12-03",
  "lastActive": "2007-12-03",
  "groups": [Group],
  "uid": "xyz789",
  "userKey": "abc123",
  "customRegistrationQuestions": [SurveyQuestionAnswer],
  "registrationDate": "2007-12-03",
  "teamId": 123,
  "visitedAt": "2007-12-03"
}
Types

BaseAccount

Fields
Field Name Description
id - ID! The ID of the Account object from the table.
features - [String]! A list of the features that are enabled for the account.
name - String! The name of the Account. Normally the name of the company.
slug - String! This is the clients slug for the account. This is used for the URL to the account.
Example
{
  "id": 4,
  "features": ["xyz789"],
  "name": "xyz789",
  "slug": "xyz789"
}
Types

BaseTeam

Fields
Field Name Description
id - ID!
name - String!
Example
{
  "id": "4",
  "name": "abc123"
}
Types

Boolean

Description

The Boolean scalar type represents true or false.

Example
true
Types

CacheControlScope

Values
Enum Value Description

PUBLIC

PRIVATE
Example
"PUBLIC"
Types

CategorySale

Fields
Field Name Description
coupon - String
datePurchased - Date!
discount - Float!
userId - Int! The userId field is the same as id, but easier to utilize for cross referencing in other systems
email - String! A users email as inputted in Path
username - String
firstName - String! A users first name as inputted in Path
lastName - String! A users last name as inputted in Path
language - String! The designated language for translations and text rendering in Path for a user.
countryCode - String The country a user designated in their Path profile
stateCode - String The state a user designated in their Path user profile
timeZone - String The timezone a user is in and is selected on the Path user profile.
firstLogin - Date When the user first signed into their Path account. Activity dates and timestamps are shown in Pacific Time (US & Canada)
lastLogin - Date When the user last signed into their Path account. Activity dates and timestamps are shown in Pacific Time (US & Canada)
lastActive - Date When the user was last signed into their Path account. Activity dates and timestamps are shown in Pacific Time (US & Canada)
groups - [Group] This is a list of groups a user is designated to in on an account.
uid - String
userKey - String
customRegistrationQuestions - [SurveyQuestionAnswer]! If an account has custom login questions configured, these records are useful to show a users answers during the login process.
itemName - String!
itemType - String!
orderId - Int!
originalPrice - Float!
price - Float!
Example
{
  "coupon": "abc123",
  "datePurchased": "2007-12-03",
  "discount": 123.45,
  "userId": 987,
  "email": "abc123",
  "username": "xyz789",
  "firstName": "abc123",
  "lastName": "abc123",
  "language": "xyz789",
  "countryCode": "xyz789",
  "stateCode": "abc123",
  "timeZone": "abc123",
  "firstLogin": "2007-12-03",
  "lastLogin": "2007-12-03",
  "lastActive": "2007-12-03",
  "groups": [Group],
  "uid": "xyz789",
  "userKey": "xyz789",
  "customRegistrationQuestions": [SurveyQuestionAnswer],
  "itemName": "xyz789",
  "itemType": "abc123",
  "orderId": 987,
  "originalPrice": 987.65,
  "price": 123.45
}
Types

Certificate

Fields
Field Name Description
id - ID! The Certificate Id
name - String The Certificate name
courseId - Int The Course Id the Certificate belongs to
courseAndSection - String The Course Section name the Certificate belongs to
metadataValues - [MetadataValue] The Certificate metadata
accountId - Int! The account Id is the associated account to the Certificate
teamId - Int The team Id is the team that the certificate report is runnning for
archivedAt - Date The date the Certificate was archived
awardCount - Int The number of certificates awared by users within the StartDate - EndDate time range
Arguments
startDate - Date!
endDate - Date!
createdAt - Date The date the Certificate was created
deletedAt - Date The date the Certificate was disabled
itemCount - Int The number of items associated to the certificate
items - [CertificateItem] The sub report of items associated to the Certificate
status - String The Certificate status (active/inactive)
templateName - String The template used by the Certificate
userCertificates - [UserCertificate] The sub report of awarded certificates to users with user information
Arguments
userIds - [Int!]
uid - String
startDate - Date!
endDate - Date!
courseIds - [Int!]
externalProductIds - String The IDs of the external products associated to the Certificate
credits - [CreditsAssignedValues] The credits used to access the Certificate
formattedCredits - [String!] The credits assigned to the Certificate in a string format
Example
{
  "id": "4",
  "name": "xyz789",
  "courseId": 987,
  "courseAndSection": "abc123",
  "metadataValues": [MetadataValue],
  "accountId": 123,
  "teamId": 123,
  "archivedAt": "2007-12-03",
  "awardCount": 123,
  "createdAt": "2007-12-03",
  "deletedAt": "2007-12-03",
  "itemCount": 987,
  "items": [CertificateItem],
  "status": "abc123",
  "templateName": "xyz789",
  "userCertificates": [UserCertificate],
  "externalProductIds": "abc123",
  "credits": [CreditsAssignedValues],
  "formattedCredits": ["xyz789"]
}
Types

CertificateForUser

Fields
Field Name Description
id - ID! The Certificate Id
name - String The Certificate name
courseId - Int The Course Id the Certificate belongs to
courseAndSection - String The Course Section name the Certificate belongs to
metadataValues - [MetadataValue] The Certificate metadata
awardedAt - Date
credits - String
Example
{
  "id": 4,
  "name": "xyz789",
  "courseId": 123,
  "courseAndSection": "xyz789",
  "metadataValues": [MetadataValue],
  "awardedAt": "2007-12-03",
  "credits": "xyz789"
}
Types

CertificateItem

Fields
Field Name Description
courseItemId - Int
id - ID!
name - String
type - String
credits - [CreditsAssignedValues]
Example
{
  "courseItemId": 123,
  "id": "4",
  "name": "abc123",
  "type": "xyz789",
  "credits": [CreditsAssignedValues]
}
Types

CertificateUser

Fields
Field Name Description
id - ID!
userId - Int! The userId field is the same as id, but easier to utilize for cross referencing in other systems
email - String! A users email as inputted in Path
username - String
firstName - String! A users first name as inputted in Path
lastName - String! A users last name as inputted in Path
language - String! The designated language for translations and text rendering in Path for a user.
countryCode - String The country a user designated in their Path profile
stateCode - String The state a user designated in their Path user profile
timeZone - String The timezone a user is in and is selected on the Path user profile.
firstLogin - Date When the user first signed into their Path account. Activity dates and timestamps are shown in Pacific Time (US & Canada)
lastLogin - Date When the user last signed into their Path account. Activity dates and timestamps are shown in Pacific Time (US & Canada)
lastActive - Date When the user was last signed into their Path account. Activity dates and timestamps are shown in Pacific Time (US & Canada)
groups - [Group] This is a list of groups a user is designated to in on an account.
uid - String
userKey - String
customRegistrationQuestions - [SurveyQuestionAnswer]! If an account has custom login questions configured, these records are useful to show a users answers during the login process.
registrationDate - Date! The date a user created an account in Path, accepted a registration link or was imported into Path
admin - Boolean
accountId - Int
adminAccessLevels - [String]
Example
{
  "id": "4",
  "userId": 987,
  "email": "xyz789",
  "username": "abc123",
  "firstName": "xyz789",
  "lastName": "xyz789",
  "language": "abc123",
  "countryCode": "xyz789",
  "stateCode": "xyz789",
  "timeZone": "abc123",
  "firstLogin": "2007-12-03",
  "lastLogin": "2007-12-03",
  "lastActive": "2007-12-03",
  "groups": [Group],
  "uid": "xyz789",
  "userKey": "abc123",
  "customRegistrationQuestions": [SurveyQuestionAnswer],
  "registrationDate": "2007-12-03",
  "admin": false,
  "accountId": 123,
  "adminAccessLevels": ["abc123"]
}
Types

CertifiedCredit

Fields
Field Name Description
id - ID! A users database record id
userId - Int! The userId field is the same as id, but easier to utilize for cross referencing in other systems
email - String! A users email as inputted in Path
username - String
firstName - String! A users first name as inputted in Path
lastName - String! A users last name as inputted in Path
language - String! The designated language for translations and text rendering in Path for a user.
countryCode - String The country a user designated in their Path profile
stateCode - String The state a user designated in their Path user profile
timeZone - String The timezone a user is in and is selected on the Path user profile.
firstLogin - Date When the user first signed into their Path account. Activity dates and timestamps are shown in Pacific Time (US & Canada)
lastLogin - Date When the user last signed into their Path account. Activity dates and timestamps are shown in Pacific Time (US & Canada)
lastActive - Date When the user was last signed into their Path account. Activity dates and timestamps are shown in Pacific Time (US & Canada)
groups - [Group] This is a list of groups a user is designated to in on an account.
uid - String
userKey - String
customRegistrationQuestions - [SurveyQuestionAnswer]! If an account has custom login questions configured, these records are useful to show a users answers during the login process.
registrationDate - Date The date a user created an account in Path, accepted a registration link or was imported into Path
teamId - Int The associated team Id if the user is a team member
creditTypesForCreditsCertified - [CreditTypeForCreditCertified]!
Arguments
startDate - Date!
endDate - Date!
certificatesCount - Int!
Arguments
startDate - Date!
endDate - Date!
freeCleUsed - Float
Arguments
startDate - Date!
endDate - Date!
certificates - [CertificateForUser]!
Arguments
startDate - Date!
endDate - Date!
Example
{
  "id": "4",
  "userId": 123,
  "email": "xyz789",
  "username": "abc123",
  "firstName": "abc123",
  "lastName": "abc123",
  "language": "xyz789",
  "countryCode": "abc123",
  "stateCode": "xyz789",
  "timeZone": "xyz789",
  "firstLogin": "2007-12-03",
  "lastLogin": "2007-12-03",
  "lastActive": "2007-12-03",
  "groups": [Group],
  "uid": "abc123",
  "userKey": "xyz789",
  "customRegistrationQuestions": [SurveyQuestionAnswer],
  "registrationDate": "2007-12-03",
  "teamId": 987,
  "creditTypesForCreditsCertified": [
    CreditTypeForCreditCertified
  ],
  "certificatesCount": 123,
  "freeCleUsed": 123.45,
  "certificates": [CertificateForUser]
}
Types

Completion

Fields
Field Name Description
id - ID!
score - Int
passed - Boolean!
Example
{"id": 4, "score": 987, "passed": false}
Types

ContentSale

Fields
Field Name Description
name - String! The content name
type - String! The content type
id - Int! The content id
price - Float The content price
status - String! The content status (active/inactive)
sales - Int! The number of sales for that content
revenue - Float! The total revenue for that content
freeCleSaleCount - Int The FreeCle sale count
salesContentDetailsKey - String! The unique key needed to get sales details
Example
{
  "name": "abc123",
  "type": "abc123",
  "id": 123,
  "price": 123.45,
  "status": "abc123",
  "sales": 123,
  "revenue": 987.65,
  "freeCleSaleCount": 123,
  "salesContentDetailsKey": "abc123"
}
Types

ContentSaleOrderItem

Fields
Field Name Description
user - User
orderId - Int!
date - Date!
price - Float!
pricePaid - Float!
refundedTotal - Float!
revoked - Boolean!
expirationDate - Date!
priceLabels - String!
paid - Boolean!
Example
{
  "user": User,
  "orderId": 987,
  "date": "2007-12-03",
  "price": 123.45,
  "pricePaid": 987.65,
  "refundedTotal": 123.45,
  "revoked": true,
  "expirationDate": "2007-12-03",
  "priceLabels": "abc123",
  "paid": true
}
Types

Coupon

Fields
Field Name Description
accountId - Int! The Account Id for the account that owns the Coupon
active - Boolean! The Coupon state. True for active or false for inactive
amount - String! The Coupon amount
code - String The Coupon code
couponType - String The Coupon type. values could be logged_in or entire
endDate - Date The Coupon date of expiration
id - ID! The Coupon Id
name - String The Coupon name
percentage - Boolean! This defines whether the coupon was a percentage of the total value
times - Int The Coupon times it applies to the product
status - String! The Coupon status
updatedAt - Date The last time the Coupon was updated
used - String Defines whether the Coupon was used
Example
{
  "accountId": 123,
  "active": false,
  "amount": "xyz789",
  "code": "abc123",
  "couponType": "xyz789",
  "endDate": "2007-12-03",
  "id": "4",
  "name": "abc123",
  "percentage": false,
  "times": 123,
  "status": "xyz789",
  "updatedAt": "2007-12-03",
  "used": "xyz789"
}
Types

Course

Fields
Field Name Description
id - ID! The Course Id
accountId - ID! The Account Id for the account that owns the course
averageRating - Float! The average rating of the course
courseItems - [CourseItem]! The list of items with in the course (i.e. sections, assessments, presentations, links, etc.)
createdAt - Date! The date the course was created
deletedAt - Date The date the course was disabled
endDate - Date The date the course ended
courseItemViewCount - Int! The count of the visits to items within the course by users
userVisitsCount - Int! The count of the visits to the course by users
Arguments
startDate - Date!
endDate - Date!
name - String! The course name
ratingsCount - Int! The count of the ratings given to the course
startDate - Date The course start date
status - String! The course status (active, inactive)
featured - Boolean Is the course featured?
teamId - Int The team that the report belongs to will be displayed when called.
metadata - [Metadata] Course Metadata
Arguments
metadataTemplateId - Int
offset - Int
limit - Int
Example
{
  "id": 4,
  "accountId": 4,
  "averageRating": 123.45,
  "courseItems": [CourseItem],
  "createdAt": "2007-12-03",
  "deletedAt": "2007-12-03",
  "endDate": "2007-12-03",
  "courseItemViewCount": 987,
  "userVisitsCount": 123,
  "name": "abc123",
  "ratingsCount": 123,
  "startDate": "2007-12-03",
  "status": "abc123",
  "featured": true,
  "teamId": 987,
  "metadata": [Metadata]
}
Types

CourseItem

Fields
Field Name Description
